author: [CATain] Shadowmoon☠️
content: You can link multiple wallets playing SAGE to your zProfile and earn zXP from them. However, if you're asking if you can consolidate two SAGE accounts down to one for the purposes of XP migration into C4, the answer is no. We set daily limits on SAGE account XP accrual intentionally. Letting people consolidate accounts would circumvent that restriction. You will, however, have two C4 accounts that are bootstrapped with XP at the start.

reply.preview: You can link multiple wallets playing SAGE to your zProfile and earn zXP from them. However, if you're asking if you can consolidate two SAGE accounts down to one for the purposes of XP migration into C4, the answer is no. We set daily limits on SAGE account XP accrual intentionally. Letting people consolidate accounts would circumvent that restriction. You will, however, have two C4 accounts that are bootstrapped with XP at the start.

author: AST [STAR]
content: @SW4GN3R there is one issue regarding C4 PTR that worries me a lot if it isn’t well focused in the future: the overwhelming complexity of the game. It’s neither good nor bad that a game is more or less complicated, what matters is how it manages to engage the player in the plot. Either through missions that make you learn dynamics or through classic tutorials (less effective and more boring). I think it’s an undeniable priority that this issue must be treated with the highest importance, because C4 can be a technological feat on-chain, but if it isn’t engaging AND addictive it will never succeed. Let's imagine that there is no incentive in the game, just the pleasure of spending some time playing, would there be many players willing to spend hours and hours in front of the screen just for pleasure? I think that right now the answer is a resounding NO, there is great potential in everything presented in the TH, but it’s of extreme importance to make it addictive for the players

author: ROME | Earther [ROME]
content: Oh yeah, this is a given. My disclaimer at the beginning that this isn't a launch of a game maybe wasn't clear enough. Imagine if someone explained and showed you every single thing you can do and interact with in Crimson Desert all within 1 hour. Your brain would melt through your ears. Now instead what if no one explained anything and you just started playing it from the start. That's the proper player journey, what we showed and what's playable now isn't tuned for a player journey. It's tuned for testing all game mechanics. If we had you testing the player journey no one would make it to the end game within out testing window and we won't know if end game is borked or not. So, some people may not be used to what QA testers have access to when they test these games, but it's a very different experience than a FTUE (first time user experience) test, which this is not. Later in PTR we will have some FTUE journeys where players won't get large airdrops of advance game assets (atm, they're getting the most powerful ship in the game immediately). So, would appreciate more FTUE styled feedback once the player journey is actually there.

Now instead what if no one explained anything and you just started playing it from the start. That's the proper player journey, what we showed and what's playable now isn't tuned for a player journey. It's tuned for testing all game mechanics. If we had you testing the player journey no one would make it to the end game within out testing window and we won't know if end game is borked or not. So, some people may not be used to what QA testers have access to when they test these games, but it's a very different experience than a FTUE (first time user experience) test, which this is not. Later in PTR we will have some FTUE journeys where players won't get large airdrops of advance game assets (atm, they're getting the most powerful ship in the game immediately). So, would appreciate more FTUE styled feedback once the player journey is actually there.

author: [SAG] Swift42 [CODE]
content:
Regarding "C4 is complex":
This is actually a good thing.
The main problem currently is that the game just throws you into the experience without explaining anything.
But this is solvable and since it needs a lot of time, it just wasn't a top priority. You need the game (and in the best case also player feedback) anyway before you can think of UI improvements and tutorials.
So I'm pretty sure we will see these things in the upcoming months.

author: Definitely_Not_JayWalk
content: Game is great and I am loving it...putting that out there first.  However one of the biggest disappointments is how small the world feels because of travel times.  You created this monster of a map with all these systems, but it only takes about an hour to subwarp across an entire faction.  Moving to deep space should be a tremendous effort.  I would have expected 24hr+ times to relocate so far away.  The map just feels WAY too small because of the travel times.  Please make them a LOT longer...it's an easy fix to make the map feel soooo much larger.
